description: Intel 80386 instruction additions. The 80386 was Intel's first 32-bit processor. It introduced 32-bit registers (EAX, EBX, etc.), paging, and a large set of new instructions including sign/zero-extend moves, bit operations, double-precision shifts, and additional segment-register load instructions.

opcodes.description: Load far pointer into FS:reg (FS = mem segment, reg = mem offset)

instructions.description: Loads a far pointer from memory into FS:reg.
instructions.notes: Segmentation is not common in modern OSes.

opcodes.description: Load far pointer into GS:reg (GS = mem segment, reg = mem offset)

instructions.description: Loads a far pointer from memory into GS:reg.

opcodes.description: Load far pointer into SS:reg (SS = mem segment, reg = mem offset)

instructions.description: Loads a far pointer from memory into SS:reg.

opcodes.description: Sign-extend byte r/m8 into word/dword register
Sign-extend word r/m16 into dword register

instructions.description: Sign-extends a byte or word into a larger register (e.g., byte→word, word→dword).
instructions.notes: In 64-bit mode REX.W can extend the destination to 64 bits. MOVSXD (opcode 0x63) sign-extends 32-bit to 64-bit.

opcodes.description: Zero-extend byte r/m8 into word/dword register
Zero-extend word r/m16 into dword register

instructions.description: Zero-extends a byte or word into a larger register.
instructions.notes: In 64-bit mode REX.W can extend the destination to 64 bits.

opcodes.description: Two-operand signed multiply: r16/32 *= r/m16/32
Three-operand signed multiply: r = r/m × imm16/32
Three-operand signed multiply: r = r/m × sign-extended imm8

instructions.description: Two- and three-operand forms of signed multiply added in 80386 (one-operand form existed in 8086).

opcodes.description: Bit Test: copy bit (r16/32) of r/m16/32 into CF
Bit Test: copy bit (imm8) of r/m16/32 into CF

instructions.description: Bit Test: copies the specified bit of the source to CF.

opcodes.description: Bit Test and Set: copy bit to CF then set it to 1
Bit Test and Set (immediate bit index)

instructions.description: Bit Test and Set: copies the specified bit to CF, then sets it to 1.

opcodes.description: Bit Test and Reset: copy bit to CF then clear it to 0
Bit Test and Reset (immediate bit index)

instructions.description: Bit Test and Reset: copies the specified bit to CF, then clears it to 0.

opcodes.description: Bit Test and Complement: copy bit to CF then complement it
Bit Test and Complement (immediate bit index)

instructions.description: Bit Test and Complement: copies the specified bit to CF, then complements it.

opcodes.description: Bit Scan Forward: find lowest set bit in src; store index in dst; ZF=1 if src=0

instructions.description: Bit Scan Forward: scans src from bit 0 upward and stores the position of the first set bit in dst. Sets ZF if src is zero.
instructions.notes: Consider TZCNT (BMI1) on modern CPUs for well-defined zero-input behavior.

opcodes.description: Bit Scan Reverse: find highest set bit in src; store index in dst

instructions.description: Bit Scan Reverse: scans src from the MSB downward and stores the position of the highest set bit in dst.
instructions.notes: Consider LZCNT (LZCNT feature flag) on modern CPUs.

opcodes.description: Double-precision shift left: shift dst left by imm8, filling from MSB of src
Double-precision shift left by CL

instructions.description: Double Precision Shift Left: shifts dst left while shifting bits in from src (used for multi-precision shifts).

opcodes.description: Double-precision shift right: shift dst right by imm8, filling from LSB of src
Double-precision shift right by CL

instructions.description: Double Precision Shift Right: shifts dst right while shifting bits in from src.
